description: User reports VPN disconnects every 15-20 minutes when working from home. Using GlobalProtect client v6.1 on Windows 11. ISP is Comcast, stable on speed tests. Issue started after the latest Windows update KB5034441.
resolution: Root cause: Windows update KB5034441 introduced a conflict with GlobalProtect's split-tunnel configuration. Fix: rolled back the specific update via DISM, then applied GlobalProtect v6.2 patch which resolves the compatibility issue. Also adjusted the gateway timeout from 30s to 60s in the GP portal config to handle brief network blips. User stable for 3 days after fix.
notes: Affected 4 other users at Meridian on the same Windows build. Applied the same fix fleet-wide via SCCM. Flagged to Microsoft via support case #MS-20260115.

description: Multiple users at Apex cannot reach internal wiki (wiki.apex-quant.internal). External sites work fine. Issue affects all users in the NYC office but not remote workers on VPN.
resolution: Internal DNS server (10.10.1.5) had a stale zone file for apex-quant.internal after a failed zone transfer at 03:00 AM. Forced a zone transfer from the primary DNS (10.10.1.2), flushed DNS cache on the secondary, and restarted the DNS service. All users confirmed resolution within 10 minutes. Added monitoring alert for zone transfer failures.
notes: The primary DNS server had its firewall rule for zone transfers accidentally removed during last week's firewall audit. Re-added rule and documented in change log.

description: Trading desk reports Bloomberg terminal cannot connect to Bloomberg B-PIPE feed. Connection was working yesterday. Error: 'Unable to establish secure connection to feed handler.'
resolution: Palo Alto firewall rule for Bloomberg B-PIPE ports (8194-8196) was inadvertently disabled during scheduled firewall maintenance window last night. Re-enabled the rule, verified traffic flow with packet capture, confirmed Bloomberg feed reconnected within 2 minutes. Added the Bloomberg rules to the 'protected rules' group that requires manager approval before modification.
notes: Critical severity - trading desk was unable to receive live market data for 47 minutes. Post-incident review scheduled. Firewall change process to be updated to require pre/post validation of critical trading system connectivity.

description: Conference room B WiFi consistently drops during video calls. Users report 2-3 disconnections per hour-long meeting. Affects both Zoom and Teams. Wired ethernet in the same room works perfectly.
resolution: Wireless AP in conference room B (Meraki MR46) was operating on a congested 2.4GHz channel with 12 neighboring networks visible. Reconfigured to force 5GHz band, set channel width to 40MHz on channel 149, and increased minimum RSSI to -70dBm to prevent clients from holding onto weak signals. Also discovered the AP was mounted behind a metal whiteboard frame causing signal reflection. Relocated AP to ceiling mount position. Post-fix: zero drops in 3-day monitoring period.
notes: Surveyed all 6 conference rooms. Rooms A and D had similar issues. Applied same fix proactively.

description: New hire at Meridian Capital cannot connect to VPN at all. Gets error 'Authentication failed - invalid certificate' on GlobalProtect. User has been provisioned in Active Directory and has a working laptop.
resolution: User's machine certificate was not auto-enrolled because the laptop was imaged with an outdated GPO template that pointed to the decommissioned CA server. Manually requested a machine certificate from the new CA (ca02.meridian.internal), installed it in the Local Machine certificate store, and restarted the GlobalProtect service. VPN connected immediately. Updated the imaging template to point to the correct CA.
notes: This is the 3rd time this quarter a new hire hit this issue. Need to audit all imaging templates. Created Jira ticket INFRA-445 to track.

description: Entire Vanguard Creek office experiencing very slow internet since this morning. Speed tests show 15 Mbps down vs normal 500 Mbps. Affects all users. Remote workers unaffected.
resolution: ISP (Zayo) confirmed a fiber cut on the primary circuit. Traffic was failing over to the backup circuit (Cogent, 50 Mbps shared) which was saturated. Contacted Zayo for ETA - repair completed at 14:30. In the meantime, implemented QoS policy on the backup circuit to prioritize trading systems and VoIP over general browsing. Post-incident: upgraded backup circuit from 50 Mbps to 200 Mbps (change order CO-2026-089).
notes: Trading systems were protected by QoS within 20 minutes of the ticket. General browsing was slow for ~4 hours until primary circuit restored. Backup circuit upgrade to be completed by Feb 15.

description: Apex users report that Confluence loads very slowly (30+ seconds per page). Other internal sites are fast. Confluence server itself shows low CPU/memory usage.
resolution: Confluence was configured to use an external CDN for static assets (fonts, CSS). DNS resolution for the CDN hostname was timing out intermittently due to the internal DNS forwarder having a stale entry. Cleared the forwarder cache, added the CDN IP range to the local DNS hosts file as a fallback, and set TTL to 3600s. Page load times dropped from 30s to under 2s. Long-term fix: configured Confluence to serve static assets locally instead of via CDN.
notes: Confluence admin had enabled CDN during a performance optimization attempt last month without notifying the network team. Added Confluence infrastructure changes to the change review board.

description: Risk management team at Ironclad cannot access the new third-party risk analytics platform (riskvault.io). Browser shows 'Connection timed out.' Platform vendor confirms their side is operational.
resolution: The riskvault.io IP range (203.0.113.0/24) was not in the firewall allowlist. Added outbound HTTPS (443) rule for the IP range in the Palo Alto policy. Also added the riskvault.io SSL certificate to the SSL decryption exclusion list since the platform uses certificate pinning and our SSL inspection was breaking the connection. Verified full connectivity including API endpoints that the risk team uses for automated data pulls.
notes: Vendor onboarding checklist updated to include firewall rule request as a mandatory step. This is the 5th time a new vendor was blocked by the firewall because the onboarding process skipped IT.

description: Silverline Advisors London office: all 12 users lost VPN connectivity simultaneously at 14:00 GMT. Users can reach the internet but not the NY data center resources via VPN tunnel.
resolution: The IPSec tunnel between London Meraki MX and NY Palo Alto was down. Phase 2 SA had expired and failed to renegotiate because the pre-shared key had been rotated on the NY side during last weekend's maintenance but the London MX was not updated. Applied the new PSK to the London MX, tunnel re-established within 30 seconds. All 12 users confirmed connectivity restored. Added the London office to the PSK rotation runbook which was previously missing it.
notes: London office was added only 3 months ago and several runbooks don't include it yet. Created task to audit all runbooks for London inclusion.

description: Meridian Capital trading desk reports increased latency to CME exchange. Normal round-trip is 2ms, currently seeing 15-25ms. Started approximately 2 hours ago. No network changes on our side.
resolution: Traceroute showed an additional 3 hops through a Level3/Lumen transit network that wasn't in the normal path. Our ISP (Zayo) confirmed a BGP route change due to maintenance on the direct peering link with CME's network provider. Contacted Zayo to request traffic engineering to restore the direct path. As interim fix, enabled the dedicated market data circuit (Colt) for CME traffic which restored latency to 3ms. Primary path restored by Zayo at 16:00.
notes: Dedicated Colt circuit proved its value today. Previously debated cutting it for cost savings. This incident justifies the expense. Trading PnL impact was minimal due to quick failover.

description: Vanguard Creek compliance team cannot access SEC EDGAR filing search. Getting 'Access Denied by proxy policy' error. They need it for regulatory filing research.
resolution: The Zscaler proxy was blocking sec.gov/cgi-bin/ URLs because the URL category was misclassified as 'Dynamic Content' which falls under the restricted category for this user group. Created a custom URL category for SEC/regulatory sites (sec.gov, finra.org, cftc.gov) and added it to the compliance team's allowed list. Verified access to EDGAR full-text search, filing downloads, and XBRL viewer.
notes: Compliance team provided a list of 15 regulatory sites they need access to. Added all to the custom category. Review scheduled quarterly to keep the list current.

description: Apex Quantitative Fund server room: network switch (Cisco 9300, rack A, position 14) showing amber LED on ports 1-12. Connected servers reporting intermittent packet loss.
resolution: Switch diagnostics showed a failing power supply unit (PSU-1). The switch has dual PSUs but PSU-1 failure was causing intermittent voltage drops to the line cards serving ports 1-12. Replaced PSU-1 with spare from inventory (part# PWR-C1-1100WAC). All port LEDs returned to green, packet loss resolved. Ordered replacement spare to replenish inventory (PO-2026-0234).
notes: Switch is 4 years old, approaching EOL. Added to hardware refresh plan for Q3 2026. Both PSUs should be monitored via SNMP trap going forward - configured alert in SolarWinds.

description: Ironclad portfolio manager cannot launch Charles River IMS. Application crashes immediately after login with error 'CRDM connection timeout'. Was working yesterday.
resolution: Charles River IMS database server had a full transaction log (SQL Server). The log file had grown to fill the entire 500GB data drive. Freed space by backing up and truncating the transaction log. Root cause: a nightly maintenance job that truncates the log had been disabled 2 weeks ago when the DBA was troubleshooting an unrelated issue and forgot to re-enable it. Re-enabled the maintenance job, verified CRDM connectivity, PM confirmed application working normally.
notes: Set up disk space monitoring alert at 80% threshold for all database servers. The existing alert was set to 95% which didn't give enough lead time for a 500GB drive.

description: Silverline Advisors: 6 users report Outlook keeps asking for password every 30 minutes. Happens on both desktop and mobile. Started after the IT team enabled Conditional Access policies last Friday.
resolution: New Conditional Access policy required compliant devices only, but 6 users had Intune enrollment issues causing their devices to report as non-compliant. The policy was falling back to basic auth prompt every 30 min. Fixed Intune enrollment by re-running the enrollment wizard and verifying compliance status in Endpoint Manager. All 6 devices now show 'Compliant'. Also adjusted the Conditional Access policy to use a 12-hour token lifetime instead of the default 1 hour for the transition period.
notes: Created a pre-deployment checklist for future Conditional Access changes: verify all devices in scope are Intune-enrolled and compliant BEFORE enabling the policy.

description: Meridian Capital cannot create new pages in Confluence. Existing pages load fine. Error when saving: 'Unable to save page - storage limit exceeded.' Space administrators also affected.
resolution: Confluence database had hit its licensed storage limit (50GB). Largest contributors: attachment storage (32GB) with many large PDF and Excel files uploaded by the compliance team. Cleaned up: removed 8GB of duplicate attachments using the built-in cleanup tool, archived 3 inactive spaces (saved 6GB), and requested a license upgrade from Atlassian for 100GB (approved, active within 24 hours). Immediate fix restored page creation by freeing 14GB.
notes: Implemented attachment size limit of 25MB per file. Set up quarterly storage review. Educated compliance team on using SharePoint for large file storage instead of Confluence attachments.

description: Vanguard Creek: Bloomberg terminal displaying stale pricing data. Prices are 20 minutes delayed instead of real-time. Other Bloomberg functions (messaging, news) work normally.
resolution: Bloomberg B-PIPE real-time data subscription had expired due to a failed auto-renewal. Bloomberg support confirmed the subscription lapsed on Jan 24. Finance team confirmed the PO was approved but Bloomberg billing never processed it. Re-submitted the renewal, Bloomberg activated real-time feed within 15 minutes. Set up calendar reminder for 30 days before next renewal date. Also configured a Bloomberg API monitor that alerts if data timestamps are more than 60 seconds behind real-time.
notes: Trading desk operated on delayed data for approximately 3 hours before reporting the issue. Need a proactive monitoring solution since users may not always notice delayed data. The Bloomberg API timestamp monitor should catch this in the future.

description: Apex Quantitative developers cannot push to Git repositories. Error: 'remote: HTTP Basic: Access denied. fatal: Authentication failed for Azure DevOps.' Affects all 8 developers. Pull works fine.
resolution: Azure DevOps PAT (Personal Access Token) used by the shared build service account had expired. This account was also used for the Git credential manager on developer machines because they had cloned repos using the build service URL. Generated a new PAT with 90-day expiry, updated the credential in Windows Credential Manager on all 8 developer machines. Long-term fix: migrated developers to SSH key authentication which doesn't expire. Kept PAT only for the build pipeline service account.
notes: PAT expiry monitoring added to the IT calendar. SSH key setup guide created and shared with the dev team. Previous wiki page for Git setup updated to recommend SSH over HTTPS.

description: Ironclad Asset Management CEO cannot open Excel files shared via SharePoint. Gets 'Sorry, we couldn't open the workbook' error in Excel Online. Desktop Excel opens them fine. Urgent - board meeting in 2 hours needs these files accessible on iPad.
resolution: The Excel files contained Power Query connections to an on-premises SQL Server. Excel Online cannot process Power Query connections to on-prem data sources - it only works with cloud data sources. For the immediate board meeting: exported the workbooks as static copies (values only, no Power Query) and uploaded to a separate SharePoint folder accessible via iPad. Long-term: working with the finance team to migrate the data source to Azure SQL so Power Query works in Excel Online.
notes: CEO was unaware of Excel Online limitations. Created a quick reference card for executives showing which Excel features work on iPad/web vs desktop only. Power Query migration to Azure SQL estimated at 2 weeks.

description: Silverline Advisors: all printers showing 'Offline' in Windows for 5 users on the 3rd floor. Other floors unaffected. Printers physically powered on and have network connectivity (can ping them).
resolution: Windows Print Spooler service had crashed on the print server (PRINT-SRV-03) that serves the 3rd floor. The crash was caused by a corrupted print driver for the HP LaserJet M507 that was updated last night via Windows Update. Rolled back the HP driver to the previous version, cleared the stuck print queue, restarted the Print Spooler service, and forced a Group Policy update on the 5 affected machines. Printing restored within 15 minutes. Blocked the HP driver update in WSUS to prevent recurrence.
notes: This is the second time a printer driver update from Windows Update caused spooler crashes. Implemented a policy to defer driver updates by 7 days and test on a pilot machine before allowing fleet-wide deployment.

description: Meridian Capital: Salesforce reports are timing out for the sales team. Report 'Pipeline Q1 2026' used to load in 5 seconds, now takes over 2 minutes and often times out. Other Salesforce functions are normal speed.
resolution: The report included a custom formula field that performed a SOQL subquery on the Opportunity object. Recent data import added 50,000 opportunity records which made the subquery extremely slow. Optimized: replaced the formula field with a scheduled batch Apex job that pre-calculates the values nightly and stores them in a custom field. Report now loads in 3 seconds. Also added an index on the Opportunity.CloseDate field which was used in the filter criteria.
notes: Salesforce governor limits documentation shared with the sales ops team. Future report designs to be reviewed by IT before deployment to avoid similar performance issues.

description: Vanguard Creek: Veeam backup job for the file server (FS-01) has been failing for 5 consecutive nights. Error: 'Processing VM Error: Failed to prepare guest for hot backup. Cannot freeze guest file system.'
resolution: The VMware Tools on FS-01 were outdated (version 11.x vs current 12.3). The quiescing mechanism that freezes the file system for consistent snapshots requires VMware Tools 12+ for Windows Server 2022. Updated VMware Tools to 12.3.5, which required a maintenance window reboot. Backup ran successfully the following night. Also discovered that VSS writers for SQL Server (running on the same VM) were in a failed state - reset them with 'vssadmin list writers' and restarted the relevant services.
notes: Created a monthly VMware Tools compliance report. 7 other VMs also have outdated Tools versions - scheduled updates over the next 2 maintenance windows.

description: Apex Quantitative: SolarWinds monitoring shows all nodes as 'Up' but the Operations team reports they're not receiving email alerts for actual outages. Last alert email received was 3 days ago despite several brief outages logged in the system.
resolution: The SMTP relay server used by SolarWinds (smtp-relay.apex-quant.internal) had its SSL certificate expire. SolarWinds was configured to require TLS for outbound email, so all alert emails were silently failing. Renewed the certificate, installed it on the relay server, and verified email delivery by triggering a test alert. Also configured a backup alert channel via Microsoft Teams webhook so alerts continue even if email fails.
notes: Certificate expiry monitoring for internal services was not in place. Added all internal SSL certificates to the certificate monitoring dashboard with 30-day expiry warnings.

description: Ironclad: FactSet workstation showing 'License server unavailable' for 3 users. Other 7 FactSet users are working normally. The 3 affected users are all on newly re-imaged machines.
resolution: The re-imaging process installed FactSet client v14.2 but the license server requires v14.3+ for the new licensing protocol introduced in January. Updated the 3 machines to FactSet v14.3.1. Also discovered the re-imaging template was using an old FactSet MSI. Updated the template with the current version. License server connection confirmed working for all 3 users.
notes: Software version matrix for the imaging template needs quarterly review. Created a runbook entry for the re-imaging checklist to verify all application versions before deploying.

description: Silverline Advisors: Partner reports not receiving emails from external senders since yesterday afternoon. Internal emails work. The partner is expecting time-sensitive deal documents.
resolution: The partner's email address was on a new custom domain (@silverline-deals.com) that was added last week for the new fund. MX records for the new domain were configured correctly, but the Exchange Online mail flow rule had a typo in the accepted domain - it was entered as 'silverline-deal.com' (missing the 's'). Corrected the accepted domain entry. Emails that had been bouncing were resent by the external parties after we confirmed the fix. NDR (Non-Delivery Report) analysis showed 23 bounced emails over 18 hours.
notes: Added a verification step to the domain setup checklist: send test emails from external accounts to the new domain before marking setup as complete. Partner received all resent documents within the hour.

description: Meridian Capital compliance officer reports that the FINRA Gateway website is not loading correctly. Some pages show without CSS styling, buttons don't work. Other regulatory sites load fine. Issue on both Chrome and Edge.
resolution: FINRA Gateway recently updated their site to require TLS 1.3. Our Zscaler SSL inspection was terminating and re-establishing connections using TLS 1.2, which the FINRA site now rejects for certain API calls that load page components. Added finra.org to the Zscaler SSL inspection bypass list. Also verified that TLS 1.3 is enabled on all compliance team browsers (it was disabled via GPO for compatibility with an older internal app that's since been retired). Removed the restrictive GPO.
notes: The GPO disabling TLS 1.3 was applied 2 years ago for a legacy app. Nobody removed it after the app was decommissioned. Audit of legacy GPOs scheduled.

description: Vanguard Creek: internal reporting dashboard (PowerBI) shows no data since midnight. Dashboard connects to Azure SQL database. Database is accessible and has data when queried directly via SSMS.
resolution: The PowerBI gateway (on-premises data gateway) service had stopped. Windows Event Log showed the gateway crashed due to an OutOfMemoryException at 23:47 last night during a large report refresh. Increased the gateway VM memory from 8GB to 16GB, restarted the gateway service, and manually triggered a dataset refresh. Dashboard showed current data within 10 minutes. Also configured the gateway for automatic restart on failure (Recovery tab in Services).
notes: The gateway VM was originally sized for 5 reports but now serves 22. Memory upgrade is a stopgap - planning migration to PowerBI Premium capacity which eliminates the on-prem gateway requirement.

description: Meridian Capital analyst reports receiving a convincing phishing email appearing to be from the CFO requesting an urgent wire transfer. The email came from 'cfo@meridian-capital-partners.com' (note the hyphens vs the real domain 'meridiancapitalpartners.com').
resolution: Confirmed phishing via header analysis - originated from a DigitalOcean IP. Quarantined the email across all mailboxes using Exchange Online threat explorer (3 other users received the same email). Blocked the sender domain in Exchange transport rules. Reported the domain to DigitalOcean abuse team and requested takedown. No users clicked the link or replied. Sent a company-wide phishing awareness reminder with screenshots of this specific attempt. Added DMARC record for meridiancapitalpartners.com with p=reject policy to prevent spoofing of the real domain.
notes: The lookalike domain was registered 2 days ago. Set up domain monitoring via DomainTools to alert on new registrations similar to meridiancapitalpartners.com. Phishing simulation scheduled for next month.

description: Apex Quantitative Fund: 2 developers locked out after losing their MFA devices (phone stolen and phone broken). They need access to production systems for a critical deployment scheduled today.
resolution: Verified identities via video call with their manager and security questions. Issued temporary FIDO2 security keys (YubiKey 5) from our emergency stock. Registered the keys in Azure AD as their MFA method, disabled the old phone-based MFA registrations. Restored access within 30 minutes. The developers completed their deployment on time. Ordered replacement stock of FIDO2 keys (order #HW-2026-0045).
notes: Recommended all critical-access users register at least 2 MFA methods (phone + security key) so losing one doesn't cause a lockout. Updated the security policy to require dual MFA registration for users with production system access.

description: Ironclad CISO reports: a former employee's account is still active in Active Directory 3 weeks after termination. The account has not been used (no login events) but it has access to the trading system and shared drives.
resolution: Immediately disabled the AD account and changed its password. Revoked all Azure AD sessions and app consents. Reviewed sign-in logs - confirmed no logins since the termination date. Audited all group memberships and removed the account from all security groups. Identified the root cause: HR submitted the termination to the offboarding list but the automated deprovisioning workflow failed silently because the ServiceNow-AD connector had a broken API token. Fixed the connector, re-tested with a test account, confirmed automated deprovisioning works. Conducted a full audit - found 2 more stale accounts from previous months.
notes: Critical finding. Offboarding SLA is 24 hours from HR notification. This failure exposed a gap in our automation monitoring. Added a daily reconciliation report that compares HR termination list against AD account status.

description: Silverline Advisors: CrowdStrike Falcon flagged a suspicious PowerShell script execution on an analyst's workstation. Alert severity: High. Script appears to enumerate network shares and collect file listings.
resolution: Isolated the workstation from the network via CrowdStrike network containment. Analyzed the PowerShell script - it was a legitimate IT audit script that the analyst downloaded from an internal SharePoint site but ran without authorization. The script was written by a former IT contractor and was not malicious, but running unsanctioned scripts violates security policy. Released the workstation from containment, removed the script, counseled the analyst on the acceptable use policy. Reviewed the SharePoint site and removed all unsanctioned scripts. Added a CrowdStrike prevention policy to block unsigned PowerShell scripts for non-IT users.
notes: The SharePoint site contained 15 scripts from the former contractor, some with hardcoded credentials. All removed. Passwords rotated for any credentials found in the scripts.

description: Vanguard Creek: DLP alert triggered - a user attempted to upload a file named 'Q4_investor_returns.xlsx' to a personal Google Drive via Chrome. File contains client portfolio data classified as Confidential.
resolution: DLP policy correctly blocked the upload. Investigated: the user was trying to work from home and didn't realize they were logged into their personal Google account instead of the corporate one. No data left the organization. Counseled the user on data handling policy. Implemented additional DLP controls: blocked all personal cloud storage uploads (Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive personal) via Zscaler CASB policy. Allowed only corporate-sanctioned cloud storage (SharePoint, corporate OneDrive). Generated an audit report of all DLP events in the past 30 days - this was the only attempted exfiltration.
notes: User was not acting maliciously - genuine mistake. However, this exposed that personal cloud storage was accessible. The new CASB policy prevents this for all users. Compliance team notified as required by the data handling policy.

description: Meridian Capital: users getting 'Your connection is not private' SSL warning when accessing the internal HR portal (hr.meridian.internal). Chrome shows NET::ERR_CERT_DATE_INVALID.
resolution: The internal CA-issued SSL certificate for hr.meridian.internal expired yesterday. Auto-renewal failed because the ACME client on the HR portal server couldn't reach the internal CA - the server's DNS was pointed to an external DNS server that couldn't resolve the internal CA hostname. Fixed DNS configuration to use internal DNS, manually requested and installed a new certificate with 2-year validity. Configured the ACME client for auto-renewal with a 30-day pre-expiry window. Added the certificate to the monitoring dashboard.
notes: This is the second internal certificate expiry incident this quarter (first was the SMTP relay in TKT-022). The certificate monitoring dashboard now covers 47 internal certificates. Remaining gap: 12 certificates on legacy systems that don't support ACME - these require manual renewal tracking.

description: Apex Quantitative: quarterly vulnerability scan (Nessus) found 3 Critical CVEs on the Jenkins build server. CVE-2024-23897 (arbitrary file read), CVE-2024-23898 (WebSocket CSRF), and CVE-2024-23899 (Git server SSRF). Jenkins version 2.426.
resolution: Upgraded Jenkins from 2.426 to 2.440.1 (latest LTS) which patches all 3 CVEs. Tested the upgrade in the staging environment first - verified all 34 build pipelines still work. Applied the upgrade during the maintenance window (Saturday 02:00). Post-upgrade scan confirmed all 3 CVEs resolved. Also enabled Jenkins CSRF protection and disabled the CLI over WebSocket as additional hardening. Reviewed the scan for other findings - 12 Medium findings logged for remediation in the next sprint.
notes: Jenkins was 2 LTS versions behind. Implemented a policy to apply LTS updates within 30 days of release. Added Jenkins version monitoring to the security dashboard.

description: Ironclad Asset Management: 3 users report receiving SMS messages claiming to be from IT support asking them to verify their credentials via a link. The link goes to 'ironclad-verify.com' which is not an Ironclad domain.
resolution: Confirmed smishing (SMS phishing) attack. Verified no users clicked the link by checking DNS logs and proxy logs for ironclad-verify.com - no hits. Reported the domain to the registrar and requested takedown. Sent company-wide alert via Teams and email warning about the SMS attack with screenshots. Contacted the mobile carrier to report the source phone numbers. Implemented a policy reminder that IT will never ask for credentials via SMS. Added ironclad-verify.com to the Zscaler block list as a precaution.
notes: Phone numbers used in the attack were traced to prepaid SIMs. Likely sourced employee phone numbers from a LinkedIn scraping tool. Reminded all staff to set their LinkedIn phone numbers to private. This is the first smishing attack targeting Ironclad.

description: Silverline Advisors: new intern in the research department was accidentally granted admin access to the production trading system during onboarding. Discovered during routine access review.
resolution: Immediately revoked the admin access. Audit log review: the intern had logged into the trading system 4 times but only performed read operations (viewing dashboards) - no trades executed, no configuration changes. Root cause: the onboarding template in ServiceNow had a dropdown where 'Research Analyst' and 'Research Admin' were adjacent options. The HR coordinator selected the wrong one. Fix: renamed 'Research Admin' to 'ADMIN - Research System Administrator (IT ONLY)' to make it clearly distinct. Added a validation rule that requires IT manager approval for any role containing 'Admin'. Generated an audit report for compliance documentation.
notes: Compliance officer notified per policy. No regulatory reporting required since no unauthorized actions were taken. Access review frequency increased from quarterly to monthly for all admin-level roles.

description: Vanguard Creek: CrowdStrike detected and blocked a ransomware attempt (LockBit 3.0 variant) on a workstation in the operations department. User had opened an Excel file emailed from an unknown sender that contained a malicious macro.
resolution: CrowdStrike successfully blocked the ransomware execution at the process level - no files were encrypted. Isolated the workstation from the network. Performed full forensic analysis: the malicious Excel macro attempted to download a second-stage payload from 185.220.101.x but the download was blocked by Zscaler. No lateral movement detected. Wiped and re-imaged the workstation. Blocked the sender domain and IP range in Exchange and Zscaler. Implemented an Exchange transport rule to block all Excel files with macros from external senders - users must request files be re-sent as .xlsx (macro-free) format. Conducted emergency security briefing for all staff.
notes: CrowdStrike prevented what could have been a significant incident. The user bypassed the 'Enable Macros' warning despite previous training. Macro blocking policy for external files is the most effective prevention. Reported to FBI IC3 and shared IOCs with FS-ISAC (Financial Services ISAC).

description: Meridian Capital: external auditor requests evidence of user access reviews for the past 12 months as part of SOC 2 Type II audit. Need access review records for all systems handling client financial data.
resolution: Compiled access review evidence from: (1) Azure AD quarterly access reviews - exported reports showing all reviews completed on schedule with reviewer sign-off, (2) Trading system access - exported from ServiceNow showing quarterly reviews by the trading desk manager, (3) File share permissions - generated from StealthAUDIT showing reviewed permissions. Found a gap: Q2 access review for the Salesforce CRM was completed 2 weeks late. Documented the exception with root cause (reviewer was on medical leave) and showed the review was completed upon return. Packaged all evidence in the auditor's requested format with a control matrix mapping.
notes: SOC 2 audit runs annually. Created an automated reminder system for access review deadlines with escalation to management if overdue by 3 days. All evidence now stored in a centralized audit repository on SharePoint.

description: Apex Quantitative: discovered that backups stored on the offsite NAS are not encrypted. Backup data includes trading strategies, client PII, and source code for proprietary algorithms. The NAS is in a locked cage at the colo facility but data-at-rest encryption was never enabled.
resolution: Enabled AES-256 encryption on the Synology NAS volume. This required: (1) purchasing and installing an encryption key management appliance, (2) migrating 4.2TB of backup data to a new encrypted volume (took 18 hours), (3) verifying all backup jobs write to the encrypted volume, (4) testing restore from encrypted backups. Also implemented: BitLocker on all backup staging servers, TLS 1.3 for backup data in transit, and encryption key escrow in Azure Key Vault. Updated the backup policy to require encryption at rest for all backup destinations.
notes: Gap existed since the NAS was deployed 2 years ago. Previous IT team did not enable encryption. Full encryption audit of all data storage locations completed - NAS was the only unencrypted store. Compliance officer notified. No evidence of unauthorized access to the NAS.

description: Ironclad trader reports one of their 4 monitors flickering intermittently. It's the rightmost Dell U2722D connected via USB-C. Flickering started 2 days ago and is getting worse.
resolution: Tested with a known-good USB-C cable - flickering stopped. The original cable had a damaged connector pin (pin 4 bent). Replaced the cable. Also updated the monitor firmware from v1.0 to v1.3 which fixed a known flickering issue with USB-C alternate mode at 60Hz. Confirmed stable operation for 24 hours.
notes: USB-C cables are the #1 cause of monitor issues on trading desks. Ordered 20 spare certified cables to keep in stock. The firmware update was not in our standard build - added to the deployment checklist for new monitor installations.

description: Silverline Advisors: new Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 does not output video through the CalDigit TS4 Thunderbolt dock. Laptop screen works fine. Previous ThinkPad X1 Gen 9 worked with the same dock.
resolution: The CalDigit TS4 firmware (v1.05) had a compatibility issue with the Intel 13th gen Thunderbolt controller in the X1 Gen 11. Updated dock firmware to v1.08 (latest) which resolved the compatibility. Also updated the Thunderbolt controller driver on the laptop and the Intel graphics driver. Video output working on both connected monitors after the updates. Tested with all 3 video outputs (2x DP, 1x HDMI) - all working.
notes: We have 15 CalDigit TS4 docks across the org. Proactively updated firmware on all of them during the next maintenance window. Created a compatibility matrix for docks vs laptop models.

description: Vanguard Creek portfolio manager reports laptop (Dell Latitude 5540) is extremely slow. Boot time is over 5 minutes. Applications take 30+ seconds to open. Laptop is 18 months old.
resolution: Diagnostics showed the 256GB SSD was 98% full (only 5GB free) and had high SMART wear indicators (92% life used). The user had 120GB of local Outlook OST cache, 60GB in the Downloads folder, and 40GB of Teams cache. Immediate fix: cleaned up Downloads (user confirmed files could be deleted), reduced OST cache to 3 months, cleared Teams cache. Freed 150GB. Long-term: replaced the SSD with a 512GB Samsung 980 Pro (on-site, 30 min swap). Performance restored to normal. Configured OneDrive Files On-Demand to prevent local storage bloat.
notes: Dell Latitude 5540 was ordered with 256GB SSD which is too small for hedge fund power users. Updated the hardware spec for future orders to minimum 512GB SSD. 7 other laptops in the fleet have the same 256GB config - scheduled proactive upgrades.

description: Meridian Capital server room: APC Smart-UPS 3000 showing 'Replace Battery' warning on the LCD. UPS is providing power to the core network switches and the primary file server.
resolution: Battery self-test confirmed one of the two battery packs has failed (pack B showing 0% capacity). Replaced both battery packs with new APC RBC55 units (always replace in pairs for balanced load). After replacement: ran a full self-test (passed), verified runtime estimate (22 minutes at current load), and recalibrated the UPS. Runtime was previously showing 8 minutes with the degraded battery - now restored to 22 minutes.
notes: UPS batteries are on a 3-year replacement cycle. This one was 3.5 years old - the replacement was overdue. Added all UPS units to the PM calendar with 3-year alerts. We have 4 UPS units in the server room - the other 3 were checked and are within spec.

description: Apex Quantitative developer's mechanical keyboard (Das Keyboard 4) suddenly stopped working. No lights, no response. USB cable is firmly connected. Other USB devices work in the same port.
resolution: Keyboard internal fuse had blown (common issue with Das Keyboard 4 after power surges). This was likely caused by the user connecting the keyboard to an unpowered USB hub that back-fed voltage during a brief power fluctuation last week. Provided a replacement keyboard from inventory (same model). Advised user to connect the keyboard directly to the laptop/dock USB port, not through an unpowered hub. Disposed of the damaged keyboard and updated the asset register.
notes: This is the 3rd keyboard failure in 6 months due to unpowered USB hubs. Ordering 10 powered USB hubs (Anker 10-port) to replace all unpowered hubs on developer desks.

description: Ironclad: main boardroom Poly Studio X70 video bar is not connecting to Zoom. Shows 'No network connection' despite Ethernet cable being plugged in. Board meeting with investors in 90 minutes.
resolution: Network port in the boardroom wall was disabled in the switch config. It was disabled last week during a security audit of unused ports and the boardroom port was incorrectly flagged as unused (it's only used during meetings). Re-enabled the port, assigned it to the correct VLAN (AV VLAN 50), and verified the Poly was connecting to the Zoom Room cloud service. Tested with a test Zoom call - video, audio, and screen sharing all working. Created a separate 'Conference Room' port group in the switch config that is excluded from future unused-port audits.
notes: The investor meeting proceeded on schedule. Created a spreadsheet documenting every network port in the building with its purpose to prevent future false-positive disabling. 47 ports documented across 6 conference rooms and 3 huddle spaces.

description: Silverline Advisors: main office printer (HP Color LaserJet M577) printing with horizontal lines and faded colors. Print quality has been degrading over the past week.
resolution: Opened the printer - the cyan toner cartridge was nearly empty and the imaging drum showed visible wear marks. Replaced all 4 toner cartridges and the imaging drum unit. Ran the built-in calibration and cleaning cycle. Print quality restored to new condition. Set up HP Smart Device Services monitoring to proactively alert when toner levels drop below 20%.
notes: This printer handles approximately 5,000 pages/month. Toner replacement cycle is about 4 months at this volume. Ordered a spare set of cartridges to keep in the supply closet. Previous IT setup had no toner monitoring - users reported issues only after quality degraded significantly.

description: Vanguard Creek: HP ProLiant DL380 Gen10 in the server room showing amber health LED. iLO dashboard reports a predicted hard drive failure on Drive Bay 3 (1.2TB SAS, RAID 5 array).
resolution: SMART data confirmed Drive Bay 3 had 847 reallocated sectors and climbing. RAID 5 array was still operational (degraded) with the other 5 drives healthy. Hot-swapped the failing drive with a new identical HP 1.2TB SAS drive from inventory. RAID rebuild completed in 4 hours 12 minutes. All data intact, no downtime. Health LED returned to green. Ordered a replacement spare drive to replenish inventory.
notes: The drive was 5 years old (original equipment). Proactively checked SMART data on all other drives in the array - one more drive (Bay 1) showing elevated wear. Scheduled replacement for next maintenance window. Considering migration to RAID 6 for better fault tolerance given the age of the drives.

description: Meridian Capital analyst spilled coffee on their ThinkPad T14s. Laptop shut down immediately. Keys are sticky and screen has visible liquid behind the panel.
resolution: Laptop assessed as non-repairable (liquid damage to motherboard visible through bottom panel). Deployed a spare ThinkPad T14s from inventory within 2 hours. Restored user's profile via Intune Autopilot enrollment and OneDrive sync. All data recovered - user had 100% cloud-synced profile. Disposed of damaged laptop per asset disposal policy (drive removed and securely wiped before disposal). Insurance claim filed.
notes: We keep 3 spare laptops in inventory for exactly this scenario. Reorder triggered - spare stock now at 2. User was fully productive on the new machine within 3 hours of the spill thanks to cloud-first profile management and Autopilot.

description: Apex Quantitative: 4 users report their Jabra Evolve2 75 headsets have poor microphone quality on Teams calls. Recipients say the audio is muffled and has echo. Issue appeared after the latest Teams update.
resolution: The Teams update changed the default audio processing settings. The 'High fidelity music mode' was auto-enabled which bypasses Teams' echo cancellation and noise suppression - these features conflict with Jabra's built-in audio processing. Disabled 'High fidelity music mode' in Teams audio settings on all 4 affected machines. Also updated the Jabra firmware to v2.14 via Jabra Direct, which improved noise cancellation compatibility with the new Teams audio pipeline. Audio quality confirmed excellent by all 4 users in test calls.
notes: Teams pushed this setting silently in the update. Applied the fix proactively to all 35 Jabra headset users via a PowerShell script that modifies the Teams config file. Created a wiki page for the helpdesk documenting this issue.

description: Ironclad: new Bloomberg terminal setup requires 4 monitors but the Dell docking station WD19TBS only supports 3 external displays. Trader needs all 4 for their workflow: market data, order entry, portfolio view, and chat/email.
resolution: The WD19TBS supports maximum 3 displays via its built-in ports. Solution: added a StarTech USB-A to DisplayPort adapter (USB32DPES2) for the 4th monitor. This uses DisplayLink technology which works independently of the Thunderbolt display pipeline. Installed the DisplayLink driver, connected the 4th monitor (Dell U2722D), configured Windows display settings for the 4-monitor layout. All 4 monitors running at native 2560x1440 resolution. Bloomberg terminal confirmed working across all 4 screens.
notes: This will be the standard 4-monitor trading desk configuration going forward. Ordered 5 more USB-to-DP adapters for inventory. Updated the trading desk setup guide with the new 4-monitor wiring diagram.

description: Silverline Advisors London office: Meraki MX84 firewall fan is making loud grinding noise. The device is in a small closet with limited cooling. Current CPU temperature reading is 78°C (normal operating max is 70°C).
resolution: Shipped a replacement Meraki MX84 via overnight courier to London. Scheduled an after-hours maintenance window (22:00 GMT) to swap the device. Cloned the configuration from the failing unit to the replacement via Meraki Dashboard before shipping. On-site London contact (building facilities manager) performed the physical swap with remote guidance via Teams video call. New device came online, pulled its config from the cloud, and restored full VPN connectivity to NY within 5 minutes of power-on. Added a small USB fan to improve airflow in the closet. Temperature now reading 58°C.
notes: The London closet has no dedicated cooling. Requested building management install a ventilation fan (facilities ticket FM-2026-0078). Long-term: planning to move London network equipment to a proper small server cabinet with built-in cooling when the lease renews in Q4.
